description: The first twelve slide-level fixtures for proving NodeSlide's visual vocabulary. Fixtures are benchmark inputs, not promoted showcase artifacts.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Establish one memorable, evidence-bounded thesis before the detailed argument begins.
fixtures.prompt: Create one hero thesis slide for product leaders evaluating NodeSlide. The thesis must say that reviewable generation makes presentation automation safer, while clearly separating the measured workflow time from the broader product claim. Use one dominant visual center, no card grid, no invented adoption metrics, and no more than two supporting text elements.

evidence.label: Workflow receipt
evidence.content: A frozen product-demo fixture reached structured draft at 31 seconds, validation at 34 seconds, human acceptance at 52 seconds, and PowerPoint export at 58 seconds.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Keep the thesis and measured timing as editable text; remove decorative media before rasterizing evidence.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Make the operating verdict scannable while preserving comparison context for every metric.
fixtures.prompt: Create one executive KPI strip for a Q2 operating review. Show Q2, prior-quarter, and target context for revenue, pipeline, win rate, implementation time, and retention. Use real numeric hierarchy rather than five identical cards, visually flag only material misses, keep every number editable, and do not describe a metric as on target when the supplied target disproves it.

evidence.label: Q2 KPI export
evidence.content: revenue_musd Q1 4.2 Q2 4.8 target 5.0; pipeline_musd Q1 9.1 Q2 7.4 target 10.0; win_rate_percent Q1 24 Q2 21 target 28; implementation_days Q1 46 Q2 39 target 35; gross_retention_percent Q1 93 Q2 94 target 95

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Use an editable table with conditional emphasis if the native KPI strip cannot export.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Reveal divergence between related series without inventing a cause for the observed pattern.
fixtures.prompt: Create one editable multi-series trend slide for city mobility planners. Plot rapid bus, local bus, and rail from January through June, label the end values directly, and make local bus under-recovery the visual conclusion. Add at most one annotation grounded in the values. Do not infer commuter preference or any causal explanation that is absent from the evidence.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Export editable lines and labels; omit animation before using a static chart image.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Show a forecast and its uncertainty honestly instead of presenting one modeled value as certain.
fixtures.prompt: Create one forecast-versus-actual slide with an uncertainty band for an investment committee. Plot the observed first two periods, the base forecast, and the supplied low-to-high range for the next three periods. Label the range as modeled, keep the band and line editable, and make the widening uncertainty visible without using decorative gradients or false precision.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Use editable boundary lines plus a translucent shape when a native uncertainty band is unavailable.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Make the portable protocol boundary and backend-specific responsibilities immediately inspectable.
fixtures.prompt: Create one editable technical architecture slide showing client, portable NodeSlide protocol, validation engine, adapter boundary, and three backend adapters. Use a single left-to-right reading direction, typed connectors, and one visibly distinct trust boundary. Do not turn the architecture into prose cards, do not add services not named in the evidence, and keep every node and connector editable in PowerPoint.

evidence.label: Portable runtime contract
evidence.content: Client calls initialize, stage, commit, and restore through a portable protocol. Validation runs before commit. Memory, Convex, and Postgres adapters implement persistence behind the adapter boundary.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Prefer grouped editable shapes and connectors; never replace the architecture with paragraph arrows.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Expose ordering, review custody, and failure boundaries across one request lifecycle.
fixtures.prompt: Create one editable sequence diagram for a request moving through User, NodeSlide client, planning model, validator, and adapter. Show submit, plan, validate, review, commit, and receipt in order; show the failed-validation return path separately. Use lifelines and directional messages rather than stacked process cards, and preserve all participants and connectors as editable PowerPoint objects.

evidence.label: Lifecycle events
evidence.content: User submits brief. Client sends bounded context to planning model. Model returns typed plan. Validator either returns issues to the client or passes the proposal to human review. Only accepted proposals reach adapter commit. Adapter returns a versioned receipt.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Use editable lifelines and arrows; a static Mermaid image is not promotion-eligible.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Show the progression from research question to evidence-backed decision with honest stage timing.
fixtures.prompt: Create one research timeline slide with five dated milestones: question frozen, evidence collected, model candidates generated, blind review completed, and harness update approved. Use spacing that reflects elapsed days, emphasize the review gate, keep dates and milestone shapes editable, and do not imply that the harness update occurred before human approval.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Use editable milestone shapes and a line; remove motion before rasterizing the entire timeline.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Connect visible product controls to the claimed reviewable-generation workflow without fabricating UI evidence.
fixtures.prompt: Create one product-proof slide around the supplied NodeSlide editor screenshot placeholder. The slide must reserve one large 16:9 media region and place three numbered editable callouts for model selection, validation status, and trace inspection. Because no bitmap is supplied, label the media region as an explicit replacement-required placeholder and do not simulate a fake interface.

evidence.label: Evidence availability
evidence.content: No product screenshot bitmap is included. Verified product surfaces are model selection, validation status, and the Trace inspector.

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Keep the placeholder and callouts editable; never manufacture a product screenshot.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Let a reviewer trace one headline claim through source, extraction, validation, and slide usage.
fixtures.prompt: Create one editable evidence-lineage slide showing source record to extracted fact to bounded claim to slide element to exported receipt. Bind the measured claim 'human acceptance at 52 seconds' to its source ID, show the unsupported 'zero errors' claim rejected on a separate branch, and keep the dominant reading direction left to right without decorative node clutter.

evidence.label: Journey timing receipt
evidence.content: human_accept elapsed_seconds 52 status complete; no browser-error evidence is present

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Preserve editable nodes, connectors, source ID, and rejection state; never flatten the lineage into prose.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Explain the cost-adjusted quality measure and connect every symbol to the supplied experiment table.
fixtures.prompt: Create one equation explainer slide for applied machine-learning researchers. Render quality divided by cost as real mathematics, define Q and C next to the equation, then calculate the adaptive policy value from Q equals 0.75 and C equals 0.038. Keep the equation legible at presentation scale and ensure PowerPoint receives a rendered equation fallback rather than a raw LaTeX string.
allowedClaims: Q divided by C is approximately 19.74

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Export the rendered equation as SVG with adjacent editable definitions; raw LaTeX text fails the gate.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Pair a small API contract with measured runtime behavior so code and evidence reinforce each other.
fixtures.prompt: Create one technical proof slide pairing the supplied TypeScript interface with the measured Convex adapter latency. Keep the code excerpt syntax-aware and legible, show p50 38 milliseconds and p95 92 milliseconds as a compact runtime result, and visually connect commit to receipt bytes without claiming production proof or zero latency.

evidence.label: TypeScript contract
evidence.content: interface CaseflowAdapter { stage(input: StageInput): Promise<Proposal>; commit(input: CommitInput): Promise<Receipt>; restore(versionId: string): Promise<Receipt>; }

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Keep runtime numbers editable and export code as a syntax-highlighted SVG plus accessible source text.

fixtures.narrativeJob: Prioritize operating risks by likelihood and impact instead of presenting an unlabeled list.
fixtures.prompt: Create one executive risk matrix with labeled likelihood and impact axes, consistent one-to-five scales, and three editable risk markers. Place pipeline coverage at likelihood four and impact five, implementation capacity at three and four, and retention concentration at two and five. Add one concise decision implication and do not substitute a bar chart or linear stack.

evidence.label: Q2 risk register
evidence.content: pipeline coverage likelihood 4 impact 5; implementation capacity likelihood 3 impact 4; retention concentration likelihood 2 impact 5

artifactContract.fallbackPolicy: Use editable axes, zones, and markers; a bar chart or linear stack fails artifact-type matching.
